meta.title: Contractor Prequalification — Acme Scaffolding
meta.subtitle: Work-at-height scaffold package, Plant 3 turnaround — risk tier T3 (high-hazard)
meta.author: HSE Operations / Procurement
meta.deid_notice: The contractor's accident/enforcement history was de-identified before scoring: named injured persons are referred to by role label and any injury cell below 5 is suppressed. The re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: Prequalification of Acme Scaffolding to erect/dismantle access scaffold for the Plant 3 turnaround. The scope contains work at height, so the package is risk-tiered T3 (high-hazard) — the tier is set by the highest-risk activity, not the average (ISO 45001 §8.1.4; UK CDM 2015 reg. 8). T3 demands the full PQQ + high-hazard module and cannot pass on PPE/competence claims alone.

rows: HSE policy signed by top management
Method statements / RAMS for the scope
sections.caption: PQQ depth scales to the tier (KB-DATA-CONTRACTOR-TIERS / KB-SNIP-PQQ-BANK)
Every score traces to a VERIFIED row; an asserted claim with no evidence is [GAP], never 'met'.

rows.title: Core + task-specific criteria met on verified evidence
rows.description: Insurance, SSIP accreditation, de-identified accident history, RAMS, and trade competence are all verified against supplied evidence.

rows.title: Work-at-height certification asserted but not evidenced
rows.description: A self-asserted height-work certification with no certificate supplied is scored [GAP], not 'met'. T3 work cannot pass on a competence claim alone.

rows.title: Verified height accident-rate data not supplied
rows.description: The high-hazard module requires verifiable accident-rate data for the activity; none was supplied.

sections.text: Two [GAP] items remain: (1) in-date work-at-height certification (CISRS Advanced or equivalent) is asserted but not evidenced; (2) verified height-work accident-rate data is not supplied. A self-asserted claim is never scored as met, and a T3 package cannot pass on competence claims alone — these block an unconditional approval.

rows.action: CONDITIONAL APPROVAL — supply in-date work-at-height certification (CISRS Advanced or equivalent) for the named operatives
Supply verifiable height-work accident-rate data for the last 3 years

sections.text: Recommendation: CONDITIONAL. Do not mobilise Acme Scaffolding for work at height until both conditions are closed and re-verified. Review date: on certificate expiry, or 12 months, whichever is sooner.

This prequalification is decision-support only and must be reviewed and signed off by a competent person before the contractor is appointed. It does not assert 'approved by a competent person'. Regulatory basis: ISO 45001:2018 §8.1.4 (procurement / contractors / outsourcing); UK CDM 2015 reg. 8 (duty to appoint only those with the skills, knowledge and experience); US OSHA multi-employer worksite policy CPL 02-00-124 where a US controlling-employer.
